passage.title: Family Photo Calendar
passage.text: Our group keeps a chore strip near the family table. In the evening, Joon reads it before bath time. The chore strip has photo cards for each family job for the day's job. Dad asks us to share the work in a fair order. Sometimes we compare today's photo with yesterday's photo and notice one small change. We like the chore strip because it helps us remember who helps next.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the chore strip is used.
The first sentence says the chore strip is near the family table.

questions.prompt: When does Joon use the chore strip?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Joon uses it in the evening.

questions.prompt: What does the chore strip have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the chore strip has photo cards.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
